memoryArtifacts.headline: Historical Marker for Lawne's Creek Church, Virginia
memoryArtifacts.description: In 1624, Thomas Bennett Jr. and his wife, Agnes Beard Bennett, lived on the south side of the James River, near Lawne's Creek.  This is around the time that their children, John Bennett and Philip Bennett, were born.

zh.lifeSummary: John Bennett是Thomas Bennett和Agnes Beard的兒子，於1624年7月10日出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特維維利斯可姆。他於1643年與Mary Berry在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1668年在美國維吉尼亞州諾圖波藍德享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Bennett是Thomas Bennett和Agnes Beard的儿子，于1624年7月10日出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特維維利斯可姆。他于1643年与Mary Berry在英国薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1668年在美国弗吉尼亚州諾圖波藍德享年44岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Bennett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the medieval personal name Benedict , from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. In the 12th century the Latin form of the name is found in England alongside versions derived from the Old French form Beneit, Benoit, which was common among the Normans. The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Bernhard  .
Altered form of French Benet or Bennet or of some other similar (like-sounding) French surname. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Bennetts
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant of Bennett , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Dittus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Fritz, Kurt, Otto, Reinhold.
German: from a vernacular short form of the Latin personal name Benedictus (see Bennett ).

variants.name: Benedict
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: from the personal name Benedict, from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. This owed its popularity in the Middle Ages chiefly to Saint Benedict of Norcia (c. 480–550), who founded the Benedictine order of monks at Monte Cassino and wrote a monastic rule that formed a model for all subsequent rules. No doubt the meaning of the Latin word also contributed to its popularity as a personal name, especially in Romance countries. Occasionally the English surname may derive from Latin benedicite ‘bless (you)’, perhaps given as a nickname to an habitual user of the expression. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed the German variant Benedikt and many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Benedek , Slovenian Benedik (see Benedick ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Italian Benedetti .

variants.name: Bennight
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Bennett  .

variants.name: Bennison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Bene(i)t + -son. See Bennett and compare Benson .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: John Bennett was born on 10 July 1624, in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom as the son of Thomas Bennett and Agnes Beard. He married Mary Berry in 1643, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1668, in Northumberland, Virginia, United States, at the age of 44, and was buried in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: John Bennett est né le 10 juillet 1624, à Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Thomas Bennett et Agnes Beard. Il a épousé Mary Berry en 1643, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1668, à Northumberland, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: John Bennett è nato il il 10 luglio 1624 nel Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Thomas Bennett e Agnes Beard. Lui sposato Mary Berry nel 1643, nel Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1668, nel Northumberland, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Bennettは1624年7月10日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトウィベリスコンベでThomas BennettとAgnes Beardの息子として生まれた。1643年にイギリスソメルセトでMary Berryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1668年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州ノースウンバーランドで44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Bennett(은)는 1624년 7월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 위벨리스코브에서 Thomas Bennett(와)과 Agnes Beard의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1643년 영국 서머세트에서 Mary Berry(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1668년 미국 버지니아 주 노르스벌랜드에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: John Bennett nasceu em 10 de julho de 1624, em Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Thomas Bennett e Agnes Beard. Ele casou-se com Mary Berry em 1643, em Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1668, em Northumberland, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: John Bennett родился 10 июля 1624 г. в местности вивелискомб, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Thomas Bennett и Agnes Beard. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Berry в 1643 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1668 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: John Bennett nació el 10 de julio de 1624, en Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Thomas Bennett y Agnes Beard. Se casó con Mary Berry en 1643, en Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1668, en Northumberland, Virginia, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
